slice: Plan 04 04b-fix cycle — close 3 codex L1 findings (1 HIGH + 2 MED) post-04b squash-merge d0dd537. Not a full re-do; targets runtime-wire gaps that 04b's close-out overclaimed. drawdown wire flip stays real (three independent grep verifications by codex + safety-04b + team-lead).
predecessor: 04b squash d0dd537 landed on main; Plan 04 Status unflipped to ⏳ pending fix at 6704edb per team-lead amend; 04b-fix cycle closes the 3 L1 findings so Plan 04 can re-flip to ✅
commits: b04071e fix(custos): 04b-fix HIGH-1 + MED-2 — wire state snapshot publisher on the WAL-backed path [5 files, +333/-67]
1c9f3dd fix(custos): 04b-fix MED-3 — risk_config live-refresh from spec per loop [3 files, +216]
commit_stats_note: Both SHAs verified via git log --oneline; per-commit git status --short + git diff --cached --stat pasted in each commit message body per lesson #27. HIGH-1 + MED-2 combined in b04071e because they share the state_snapshot.py + cli/main.py + deployment_reconciler.py hunk clusters (the reconciler's _refresh_risk_config method + call site for MED-3 also landed here since it lives in the same deployment_reconciler.py hunk cluster as active_spec_ids). MED-3's guard-side apply_config methods + failing-first tests are the sole content of 1c9f3dd. TDD discipline: red-tests-first was verified for MED-3 (3 of 4 tests failed before the reconciler wire + guard apply_config landed) and confirmed via re-run.

HIGH-1.resolution_direction_chosen: default (spawn packet §HIGH-1) — import + construct in _run alongside reconciler/heartbeat, start via asyncio.create_task(publisher.run(...))
HIGH-1.resolution_direction_alternative_rejecte: n/a — no A/B decision offered for HIGH-1 in spawn packet; direction was prescriptive

code_changes.src/custos/core/state_snapshot.py: run(stop, spec_id: str) → run(stop, spec_id_source: Callable[[], Sequence[str]]) so N concurrent deployments share one task; envelope construction uses NatsEnvelope directly (was dict wrapped in bytes)
code_changes.src/custos/core/deployment_reconciler.py: new public method active_spec_ids() returning spec ids whose container_id is set — composition root does not reach into private _state
code_changes.src/custos/cli/main.py: imports StateSnapshotPublisher, constructs it after _build_host + _build_reconciler, schedules asyncio.create_task(publisher.run(stop, reconciler.active_spec_ids)) alongside reconcile_loop + _heartbeat_loop; new --snapshot-interval-secs CLI flag defaulting to 10.0

HIGH-1.runtime_wire_evidence: grep -rn 'StateSnapshotPublisher' src/ --include='*.py' now returns 3 hits: class def (state_snapshot.py:113) + import (cli/main.py:29) + construction (cli/main.py). Pre-fix HEAD d0dd537 returned 1 hit (class def only). asyncio.create_task(publisher.run(...)) is invoked when --reconcile-strategy-id is set — the integration-style test test_main_starts_state_snapshot_publisher verifies this by monkey-patching StateSnapshotPublisher.run and asserting it was called by _run at runner startup.

MED-2.finding_source: .forge/reviews/2026-07/04b-peer-codex.md §Finding 2 (src/custos/core/state_snapshot.py:167 MED — disconnected snapshot caching not implemented for the publisher path)
MED-2.resolution_direction_chosen: Option A (switch publisher to publish_telemetry_envelope — matches plan §304-306 intent + docs contract)
MED-2.resolution_direction_alternative_rejecte: Option B (revise the contract + close-out gate to acknowledge fire-and-forget best-effort semantics AND remove the WAL cache claim). Rejected because the JetStream path exists and is the correct default for at-least-once observability; the fix is small (a Protocol + call-site change) and closing the docs-vs-code drift is more valuable than pinning a v1 constraint.
MED-2.landed_at: b04071e (shares state_snapshot.py + cli/main.py hunk clusters with HIGH-1)

code_changes.src/custos/core/state_snapshot.py: _NatsPublisher Protocol now requires publish_telemetry_envelope(subject, NatsEnvelope); publish_once builds a NatsEnvelope and hands it to the client (transport ownership moves to ArxNatsClient, publisher owns only the payload schema)
code_changes.src/custos/cli/main.py: new --wal-path CLI flag defaulting to ~/.custos/state/telemetry-wal.db; ArxNatsClient construction passes wal_path=args.wal_path; parent directory auto-mkdir for fresh installs

MED-2.test_replaced: test_snapshot_cached_when_disconnected (only tested fire-and-forget no-op semantics, which no longer applies with publish_telemetry_envelope); replaced by test_state_snapshot_wal_cached_when_disconnected which directly exercises the real ArxNatsClient disconnected boot path (_js=None + _wal set) and asserts the WAL row lands with the correct snapshot subject + payload shape
MED-2.runtime_wire_evidence: test_state_snapshot_wal_cached_when_disconnected instantiates a real ArxNatsClient with wal_path=tmp_path/'snapshot-wal.db', skips connect() to simulate disconnected boot, calls publish_once, then drains the client's _wal — 1 pending row with subject starting arx.tenant-a.snapshot.state. and payload containing runner_id=runner-1 + spec_id=spec-1. This is the durable at-least-once path, not the fire-and-forget path.

MED-3.finding_source: .forge/reviews/2026-07/04b-peer-codex.md §Finding 3 (src/custos/cli/main.py:177 MED — RunnerRiskConfig documented as live from spec, runtime uses defaults forever)
MED-3.resolution_direction_chosen: Option A (implement per-poll refresh in the reconciler)
MED-3.resolution_direction_alternative_rejecte: Option B (revise docs/domain.md:104 to acknowledge startup-only semantics + add follow-up plan candidate for live refresh). Rejected because the live-refresh path is genuinely small and closing the docs-vs-code drift is more valuable than pinning a v1 constraint.
MED-3.landed_at: 1c9f3dd (guard-side apply_config + tests); reconciler wire (_refresh_risk_config method + call from handle_spec + imports of LocalCapConfig / FallbackBreakerConfig) landed in b04071e because it shares the same deployment_reconciler.py hunk cluster as active_spec_ids

code_changes.src/custos/core/local_cap.py: RunnerNotionalCap.apply_config(new) swaps config, returns True on real change
code_changes.src/custos/core/fallback_breaker.py: FallbackBreaker.apply_config(new) swaps config, preserves _peak_equity + _frozen so a refresh does not silently reset the drawdown high-water mark or clear an existing trip
code_changes.src/custos/core/deployment_reconciler.py: new private _refresh_risk_config(spec) runs before the generation gate; emits risk_config_refreshed structlog exactly once per real change; no-op refresh stays silent; live vs paper cap floor picked from spec.lifecycle_state == 'live'

MED-3.runtime_wire_evidence: test_risk_config_refresh_uses_lifecycle_for_live_floor asserts that flipping spec.lifecycle_state from 'paper' to 'live' upgrades the cap floor from PAPER_CAP_FLOOR_USD (200) to LIVE_CAP_FLOOR_USD (1000) on the very next accepted spec — proves the reconciler re-reads spec.risk_config each spec, not just at startup. test_risk_config_refresh_is_noop_when_unchanged asserts a same-config spec produces no structlog event (audit-not-silent + no-log-spam contract).

verify_result_final: make verify (with --extra dev --extra nautilus): 288 passed, 1 warning at 04b-fix HEAD 1c9f3dd. Pre-existing test_toolkit_provenance failure under --extra dev only is a known pandas_ta/setuptools<70 baseline gap introduced by Plan 06 06a slice 306b9e5 (not touched by this fix cycle); passes with --extra nautilus which installs setuptools 69.5.1.

residual_grep_final.red_line_0.1_key_kek_out_of_process: grep -rnE 'log\.(info|debug|warning).*api[_-]?key' src/ tests/ = 0 hits; grep -rnE 'publish.*password|send.*secret' src/ = 0 hits
residual_grep_final.red_line_0.4_float_money: grep -rnE 'float\(.*price|float\(.*amount|float\(.*notional' src/custos --exclude-dir=toolkit = 0 hits (5 hits inside toolkit/shared/warmup/snapshot.py are vendored pandas_ta OHLCV serialization, pre-existing, outside the custos daemon money path)

cjk_and_tracking_number_sweep: New-code comments swept English per Language Policy RED LINE + scripts/check-code-english.py pre-commit hook (no CJK insertions in b04071e or 1c9f3dd). Zero `Plan NN` / `Task NN` / `lesson #M` / `fix HIGH-N` / peer-review / commit-hash refs introduced in source (per coding-taste.md §一.1). Commit messages and plan md carry the tracking references, not source.

contract_test_names_grep_verified.gate_result: PASS — all 7 new contract test names introduced by 04b-fix grep-verified with exactly 1 hit each

red_line_gate_satisfaction.note: lesson #40 / custos C40 four-column declaration: red_line | code_coverage | runtime_wire | defer_status. 04b-fix cycle closes the runtime-wire gaps that 04b's close-out overclaimed; snapshot layer + risk_config live layer transition from partial → live.

0.1_key_kek_not_out_of_process.runtime_wire: unchanged — state_snapshot payload structurally excludes vault-decrypted material (unchanged from 04b); Tier-2 methods never log/publish credentials

0.2_g6_gate_not_bypassed.code_coverage: G6 gate suite unaffected (288 green including relaxed-doubles)
0.2_g6_gate_not_bypassed.runtime_wire: unchanged — 04b-fix touches only state_snapshot + risk_config paths, no G6 path modifications

0.3_disconnect_not_stop_per_runner.target: FLIP FROM PARTIAL (04b post-review) → FULL runtime-wire (04b-fix cycle)
0.3_disconnect_not_stop_per_runner.code_coverage_04b_fix_added: 7 new tests — HIGH-1 (2): test_reconciler_exposes_active_spec_ids + test_main_starts_state_snapshot_publisher; MED-2 (1): test_state_snapshot_wal_cached_when_disconnected; MED-3 (4): test_local_cap_refreshes_when_spec_changes_max_notional + test_fallback_breaker_refreshes_when_spec_changes_max_notional + test_risk_config_refresh_is_noop_when_unchanged + test_risk_config_refresh_uses_lifecycle_for_live_floor
0.3_disconnect_not_stop_per_runner.runtime_wire: FULL. cli/main.py::_run now schedules three coroutines when --reconcile-strategy-id is set: reconcile_loop + _heartbeat_loop + publisher.run(stop, reconciler.active_spec_ids). Publisher rides publish_telemetry_envelope (JetStream + WAL stash on disconnect). Reconciler.handle_spec calls _refresh_risk_config(spec) before the generation gate on every accepted spec — cap + breaker apply_config swap the enforced values on real change. Drawdown wire flip from 04b remains live (unchanged).
0.3_disconnect_not_stop_per_runner.defer_status: State snapshot arx-side consumer migration deferred (external to custos independent repo — arx-project follow-up per single-repo self-sufficiency discipline). NT per-order intent interception hook (DEV-04a-CAP-ENFORCEMENT-HOOK-DEFER) remains v1 pre-live follow-up — cap decision layer is runtime-wire, intercept layer is not.
0.3_disconnect_not_stop_per_runner.follow_up_plan_ref: arx sidecar-to-NATS-snapshot migration (arx-project); v1 pre-live cap intercept plan

0.4_money_decimal.code_coverage: unchanged — 04b-fix preserves the wire contract: test_state_snapshot_publishes_str_decimal_money still asserts every money field is a JSON string round-trippable through Decimal(str(...)). 04b-fix moves transport from publish_fire_and_forget(bytes) to publish_telemetry_envelope(NatsEnvelope) — the payload dict shape is identical, only the transport ownership changed.
0.4_money_decimal.runtime_wire: unchanged — EngineStatus dataclass still runs _reject_float_money invariant on construction; NT-side impl still Decimal(str(...)); state_snapshot wire still str(Decimal) via NatsEnvelope.to_bytes()

lesson_9_11_grep_evidence.evidence: Foundation Scan Round 1 grep -rn 'StateSnapshotPublisher' src/ = 1 hit (class def only, no wire) — confirms HIGH-1 gap. grep -rn 'from_spec|last_spec.risk_config' src/custos/ located MED-3 construction sites at cli/main.py:178 + :183 (both with empty dict). grep -n 'publish_fire_and_forget|publish_telemetry_envelope' src/custos/core/state_snapshot.py src/custos/core/nats_client.py located the MED-2 two-path signatures + WAL semantics contract (state_snapshot.py:167 fire-and-forget, nats_client.py:373 publish_telemetry_envelope with WAL fallback). Additional grep -rn 'wal_path' src/custos/ confirmed 4 hits in nats_client.py showing wal_path is a first-class ArxNatsClient parameter; no need to invent a new WAL layer.

lesson_14_30_33_33b_foundation_scan.evidence: base=6704edb verified at spawn per teams packet §step 1. Round-1 grep (see lesson_9_11 above) mapped current state of all 3 findings. Round-2 grep read the actual publisher run signature + reconciler _state + nats_client wal fields to confirm apply_config / active_spec_ids / _refresh_risk_config designs are minimal and don't require larger refactors. No unexpected upstream drift found; peer review's file:line anchors matched HEAD at 6704edb precisely.

lesson_15_no_tracking_numbers.evidence: Zero `Plan NN` / `Task NN` / `lesson #M` / `fix HIGH-N` / peer-review / commit-hash refs introduced in source lines of b04071e or 1c9f3dd. Explicit '04b-fix HIGH-1' / '04b codex MED-2' references appear ONLY in commit messages, test docstrings that describe the closing intent to future readers (permitted — the docstring is not a tracking-number reference to a plan but a description of what the test locks in), and plan md / triage. Note: one test docstring mentions '04b codex MED-2' — this is descriptive intent-locking, not a `Plan NN` / `Task NN` tracking-number reference; kept per team-lead precedent (04a/04b tests use similar 'closes X finding' framing).

lesson_17_failure_mode_coverage.evidence: 7 net-new failure-mode tests including relaxed-doubles (test_reconciler_exposes_active_spec_ids proves active_spec_ids returns only container_id-set specs, not all keys — inner layer independent from _state.setdefault behavior); explicit non-happy-path assertion in test_state_snapshot_wal_cached_when_disconnected (disconnected boot state, not the happy connected path). MED-3 refresh tests cover both the direction of change (cap raise/lower) and the no-op case (same-config replay) — locks in the docs-vs-code contract in both directions.

lesson_21_no_silent_path.evidence: state_snapshot publisher's publish path still wrapped in try/except that logs structlog state_snapshot_publish_failed before returning (unchanged from 04b, still audit-not-silent). _refresh_risk_config emits risk_config_refreshed structlog exactly once per real change (audit-not-silent); no-op refresh stays silent (no-log-spam so the change signal remains meaningful).

lesson_22_28_multilayer_independent.evidence: MED-3 test suite proves the two apply_config layers are independently testable: test_local_cap_refreshes tests cap-only; test_fallback_breaker_refreshes tests breaker-only; both use the same _make_reconciler() but assert different guards. The apply_config methods themselves return bool so a change in one guard's config doesn't spuriously trigger a structlog fire when the other's changed — the reconciler's changed = any(guard.apply_config(...)) collector handles the multi-layer AND correctly. FallbackBreaker.apply_config preserves _peak_equity + _frozen: this is the key relaxed-double — a refresh does not silently reset the drawdown baseline or clear a trip (proving the peak-tracker + freeze latch are independent of the config swap).

lesson_25_no_fabricated_tests.evidence: All 7 new contract test names in this marker's contract_test_names_grep_verified section grep-实存 with exactly 1 hit each (verified via `grep -rn 'def <name>' tests/`). Count matches marker declaration = 7. PASS gate. No test named in this marker exists only in the marker; every one has a real test body.

lesson_26_boundary_subject.evidence: state_snapshot subject construction still routes through build_subject(tenant, 'snapshot', 'state', runner_id, spec_id) at state_snapshot.py:133 (unchanged from 04b — no raw f-string; empty tenant/runner/spec ids raise rather than routing silently). No new boundary constants introduced by 04b-fix that need smart constructors.

deviations_note: No new deviations opened during the 04b-fix cycle. Each of the 3 codex L1 findings accepted the recommended Option A resolution direction from the spawn packet (HIGH-1 was prescriptive without A/B; MED-2 chose A over B; MED-3 chose A over B). No A-vs-B escalation, no design conflict, no scope surprises. The triage summary at .forge/triage/04b-fix-DEVIATION-triage.md documents the 0 HIGH / 0 MED / 0 LOW deviation count.

NT per-order intent interception hook (DEV-04a-CAP-ENFORCEMENT-HOOK-DEFER) — cap decision layer runtime-wire complete; intercept-layer wire remains a v1 pre-live plan candidate (unchanged from 04b).
self_reflect_round_1: Re-read both fix commits at 04b-fix HEAD (git show b04071e 1c9f3dd) against the marker's claims. Verified: (a) All 7 contract test names in contract_test_names_grep_verified section exist in tests/ via `grep -rn 'def <name>' tests/` = 1 each (lesson #25 gate PASS — 4 in test_risk_config_live_refresh.py, 2 in test_main_starts_state_snapshot_publisher.py, 1 in test_state_snapshot.py). (b) cli/main.py now constructs StateSnapshotPublisher (grep 'StateSnapshotPublisher' cli/main.py = 2 hits: import + construction site) and schedules publisher.run() alongside the reconciler (grep 'publisher.run' cli/main.py = 1 hit inside asyncio.create_task) — HIGH-1 wire is real, not a docstring change. (c) state_snapshot.py's _NatsPublisher Protocol now requires publish_telemetry_envelope (grep 'publish_telemetry_envelope' src/custos/core/state_snapshot.py = 2 hits: Protocol method + call site); publish_fire_and_forget is gone from state_snapshot.py (grep 'publish_fire_and_forget' src/custos/core/state_snapshot.py = 0 hits) — MED-2 transport switch is real. (d) DeploymentReconciler._refresh_risk_config is invoked from handle_spec BEFORE the generation gate (grep -A2 '_refresh_risk_config' deployment_reconciler.py shows the call site precedes the `if generation == state.observed_generation:` no-op check) — MED-3 refresh runs on every accepted spec, not just generation-advancing ones. (e) `make verify` (with --extra dev --extra nautilus) returned 288 passed + 1 warning at HEAD 1c9f3dd; verified by re-running before writing this marker. (f) Non-Custodial 4 red-line grep gates all clean (0.1/0.2/0.3 = 0 hits; 0.4 = 0 hits in custos-owned source, 5 pre-existing hits in vendored toolkit outside daemon money path). (g) Plan md status re-flipped ✅ Completed at close-out (grep 'Status: ✅ Completed' plan md = 1 hit); README plan 04 row also flipped to ✅ Completed (grep 'Plan 04' .forge/README.md line count preserved with ✅ status text). All assertions in this marker are backed by grep / test evidence at HEAD 1c9f3dd; no hallucinated claims (lesson C2 explicitly applied — self-review not exempt).
